changedFiles.reason: Declared bounded readiness, compatibility, traceability, and documentation work.
Recorded implementation evidence and remaining release boundaries.
Generated active Work Item status.
Added installed repository-local production adaptation guidance.
Required explicit project-specific Coverage path review acknowledgement.
Added shellcheck while retaining verified real-stack and platform matrices.
Added the repository adoption readiness target.
Distributed the adoption readiness target.
Implemented the fail-closed production adaptation configuration gate.
Directed advisory diagnostics to the hard readiness gate.
Distributed the checker, used an installed evidence source, and printed readiness follow-up.
Enforced support tiers and narrow Japanese style rules.
Resolved shellcheck control-flow and empty CDPATH findings.
Covered all readiness failures, explicit success, placeholders, and misleading CI comments.
Verified adoption evidence references an installed local source.
Verified advisory diagnostics reference the hard gate.
Verified readiness script and Make target distribution.
Covered support-tier and Japanese style drift detection.
Published Verified and Template-only tiers and production adaptation boundary.
Published equivalent Japanese support tiers and readiness boundary.
Published equivalent Chinese support tiers and readiness boundary.
Documented readiness commands and updated the checkout action example.
Documented author_todo and hash-algorithm-neutral Git object IDs.
Corrected Japanese punctuation and narrowed the command-registry guarantee.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Readiness failures must state the concrete remediation and remain distinct from doctor warnings.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The checker lists each missing configuration and exits nonzero; doctor remains advisory.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Support claims must not exceed executable CI evidence.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Only Python, Go, Rust, and TypeScript are labeled CI Verified; nine other presets are Template-only.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Adoption evidence sources must remain available inside the installed repository.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The installer copies and references .ai/cockpit/adoption.md, verified by adoption E2E.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Japanese lint rules must be narrow, deterministic, and covered by tests.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Two exact known regressions are checked by the standard-library documentation gate with regression tests.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not hand-edit generated cockpit status.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Status was generated by lifecycle commands.

risk.detail: The hard readiness gate intentionally changes when teams may claim production adaptation is complete.

observedIssues: shellcheck initially found ambiguous SCRIPT_DIR fallback control flow; the POSIX script was corrected.
The template repository itself fails readiness because no project stack is selected and Coverage paths are not adopted for a host application.

residualRisks.detail: Public v0.4.4 still lacks current source adoption and readiness capabilities; no tag was published.
Nine presets remain Template-only without executable real-project CI evidence.
The repository-local Japanese lint is intentionally narrow and is not a full native-language style checker.

reviewReadiness.reason: Readiness, traceability, tier claims, shell lint, and factual documentation gaps are fixed; release publication and nine real-stack jobs remain follow-ups.

checkpointEvidence.detail: Readiness semantics, installed evidence, public claims, documentation facts, and CI scope reviewed.
99 tests, shellcheck, documentation metadata, release distribution, compilation, and fail-closed readiness behavior reviewed.

knownGaps: A new public release has not been published.
Flutter, Java, Android, Kotlin, Swift, Ruby, PHP, C#, and generic have no real-project CI verification.
Japanese prose lint covers known deterministic regressions rather than general naturalness.
overclaimPrevention: Describe the runtime as application-language-agnostic, preserve support tiers, and require check-ai-adoption-ready before claiming production adaptation.
